title: JAGGAER Elevator: Customer RAG + Agent Layer
body: JAGGAER Elevator is a practical AI layer built on top of the customer signals JAGGAER already has. It combines a customer-specific RAG with an agent layer that helps customer-facing teams move from fragmented information to the right next action, from implementation through renewal and expansion.

title: No usage or product reality in one view
body: Today, customer-facing teams do not have one place where account history, CRM context, usage reality, and product signals come together. Reps work across messy Salesforce data, local files, and scattered notes without a clear picture of what is actually happening with the customer.

title: Manual whitespace and uneven renewal preparation
body: Whitespace analysis is still manual, and renewal preparation is inconsistent. That means JAGGAER can miss the right expansion moment, react too late to lagging accounts, and create avoidable friction for both reps and customers.

title: An elevator to the right next floor
body: Instead of climbing through disconnected systems, Elevator brings the rep to the right floor immediately: customer context, next best action, whitespace insight, historical knowledge, and executive-ready outputs in one place.

title: Customer data + agent layer + playbooks
body: JAGGAER Elevator is built on a simple idea. If JAGGAER creates a smart RAG per customer and places a lightweight agent layer on top, customer-facing teams become more aligned, better informed, and more capable of acting at the right time. The result is stronger retention, healthier accounts, and more targeted expansion.

title: Customer RAG per customer
body: At the center of the proposal is a customer-specific RAG. It stores and retrieves the real customer story: how implementation went, why the customer is configured a certain way, what pain points emerged, what compliance challenges matter, what was recommended before, and what internal teams have learned over time.

title: Simple agents that learn over time
body: The RAG is not static. An agent layer sits on top of it and learns from customer outcomes across the base. Over time it gets better at identifying similar customers, detecting laggards, flagging risks, and generating stronger playbook formulas and next-best-action guidance.

title: Signals turned into action
body: Elevator uses JAGGAER playbooks and customer signals to decide what should happen next. Instead of leaving expansion timing, renewal preparation, and adoption recovery to guesswork, the system helps teams move earlier and with more confidence.

title: Built from what is already in front of us
body: This is intentionally practical. The proposal does not depend on a fictional future platform. It is built from systems, data, and interactions that already exist across JAGGAER and can be organized into a smarter customer operating layer.

title: What JAGGAER Elevator gives the business
body: The value of JAGGAER Elevator is not just one dashboard. It is a working layer that helps teams grow accounts, prepare faster, coordinate better, and act with more confidence.

title: Whitespace identification with timing
body: Elevator identifies whitespace across products, workflows, stakeholder groups, geographies, and use cases, then helps reps understand when to propose the right product to the right customer. This creates a smarter expansion motion than manual whitespace analysis.

title: Historical customer knowledge
body: Elevator lets reps ask direct questions and get grounded answers from the customer RAG. Example questions include: How did their implementation go? What was the last item the CSM recommended? Are they interested in Contracts AI?

title: Proactive orchestration across teams
body: Elevator orchestrates Sales, AEs, AMs, PS, Support, and CSM around proactive signals from the playbook layer. The system helps teams coordinate around the next best move instead of operating in silos.

title: Similar customers
body: laggards
note: Because the agent layer learns across the customer base, Elevator can also highlight similar customers, flag lagging accounts, surface risk patterns, and help teams learn from what success looks like elsewhere in the portfolio.

title: What powers the Customer RAG + Agent Layer
body: Elevator is powered by the systems JAGGAER already uses plus the customer interactions teams generate every day. The value comes from connecting those sources into one customer intelligence layer instead of leaving them fragmented.

title: Connected data sources
body: JAGGAER Engage, Production Data, Salesforce, Emails, Meetings, Internal Documents, Pendo, Contracts, and more all contribute to the customer RAG. The goal is not just aggregation. The goal is usable context.

title: Whitespace timing example
body: Example: low invoice capture automation could trigger a recommendation to propose AppZen. Similar logic can surface Contracts AI interest, low feature adoption, or stalled commercial expansion signals at the right moment.

title: One-click leadership summary
body: Elevator should also include an executive mode that generates an account summary for leaders with one click: current health, key risks, whitespace, recent actions, stakeholder picture, and the next recommended move.

title: Question-based retrieval for reps
body: Reps should be able to type questions directly into the system and receive grounded answers from the customer RAG instead of manually searching across systems and personal files.

col2: Supports account planning and renewal preparation

title: The business case: better revenue motion and better team execution
body: JAGGAER Elevator creates value in two ways at the same time. It helps teams save time by reducing manual reconstruction of customer context, and it supports revenue by improving whitespace timing, renewal readiness, and coordinated customer action.

title: Smarter whitespace and better timing
body: When whitespace is identified automatically and paired with signals from usage, product adoption, and account context, reps can propose the right product at the right time instead of relying on manual review.

title: Less searching
body: more informed action
value: When historical customer knowledge is available instantly through the RAG, teams spend less time chasing context and more time preparing meaningful customer interactions.

title: One coordinated customer motion
body: When playbook signals orchestrate the right teams around the right next move, handoffs improve, renewal preparation becomes more consistent, and customer frustration is reduced.
